llm_pattern: |I| increases strongly with increasing |V| across the full sweep, spanning from about 10^-103 A near |V|=0.1 V to about 2.40e-5 A at -6 V and 2.25e-5 A at +6 V. The magnitude trend is broadly similar for negative and positive bias, with small point-to-point fluctuations at higher |V|. The listed ln(|I|/V^2) values increase monotonically with |V|, from about -230 near 0.1 V to about -14.2 near 6 V.

llm_keywords.evidence: |I| ranges from 4.666e-16 A at -0.9 V down to 8.897e-103 A at -0.1 V and up to 2.399e-5 A at -6 V, indicating a span of about 97.43 decades between the minimum and maximum |I| values.
At matched |V| values, |I| is close on both sides: 6 V gives 2.399e-5 A (-6 V) versus 2.249e-5 A (+6 V), and 0.2 V gives 1.744e-54 A (-0.2 V) versus 1.731e-54 A (+0.2 V).
As |V| decreases from 6 V to 0.1 V on the negative branch, |I| drops from 2.399e-5 A to 8.897e-103 A; on the positive branch, |I| rises from 6.899e-103 A at 0.1 V to 2.249e-5 A at 6 V.
At higher positive bias, adjacent points are not strictly monotonic, for example 5.45 V: 1.856e-5 A, 5.50 V: 1.795e-5 A, 5.55 V: 1.300e-5 A, then 5.80 V: 2.238e-5 A.
ln(|I|/V^2) changes from about -230.38 at -0.1 V and -230.63 at +0.1 V to about -14.22 at -6 V and -14.29 at +6 V, showing a steady increase with |V|.
